title: Chief of Staff
office_cadence_note: StudySmarter listing states verbatim: 'City of London | Full-Time | No working from home possible'. No remote/hybrid option; full-time in-office (founder right-hand role, 'in every critical conversation'). No explicit day-count but WFH explicitly excluded = effectively 5 days in person. Verified 2026-06-10.
working_week_note: RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-11 against the user's ACTUAL dealbreaker (weekend/6-7-day work only — full-time 5-day in-office is FINE). StudySmarter listing states verbatim: 'City of London | Full-Time | No working from home possible'. This excludes REMOTE work but says NOTHING about weekends, Saturday, Sunday, or a 6/7-day week — no day-count is stated at all. 'No WFH' = in-office, NOT 6-7 days. Default to a standard Mon-Fri full-time week. The prior IN-OFFICE 'DEALBREAKER' flag was WRONG (it conflated no-WFH with weekend work). FINE — NOT a dealbreaker. Reinstated. (ZipRecruiter source 403 on re-fetch; relying on the verbatim StudySmarter quote already captured.)
key_evidence: Live; very early-stage AI-risk/governance infrastructure startup (frameworks, standards, quantification models for the AI economy). Founder-led, advisors from MIT/Stanford/Columbia/Goldman.

OUTSTANDING warm-path + screening: apply via a 300-word intro emailed directly to rajen@governr.ai (a founder), 'strong candidates hear back within 48 hours.' This is a direct founder-read with NO ATS/recruiter filter — best-in-batch on factors C and E. A sharp, tailored 300-word pitch from Ryan goes straight to a decision-maker.

Requirements skew senior/breadth-heavy: 'build a financial model, create an investor pitch, design workflows, and write strategy documents — all in the same day,' founder-level intensity, strong commercial instincts. Ryan covers pitch decks/investor updates (Minima seed + Series A support), workflow design, and strategy — but a polished standalone FINANCIAL MODEL is a stretch he hasn't explicitly evidenced. 'Chief of Staff' title is borderline-senior but is a recognised founder-adjacent generalist role, not exec-excluded.

Spike fit is partial: prizes founder-level generalist ownership (maps to Ryan's first-employee/FA scaling story) and AI-economy thesis (adjacent to his AI-tooling interest), but the domain is AI-governance/risk-quant, NOT crypto/Web3 community growth — limited direct domain pull, and the role is light on the community/GTM-zero spikes Ryan is strongest at.
Path to senior leadership (COO/Head-of) signalled; in City of London (fits relocation). No salary published — CoS at a pre-seed/seed AI co could land near or modestly above £50k with equity.
biggest_lever: Direct-to-founder 300-word email application with a 48-hour response promise — bypasses all ATS/recruiter screening, the single highest-leverage application channel in this batch.
biggest_risk: Same-day financial-modelling + investor-pitch + strategy expectation at 'founder-level intensity' may exceed Ryan's evidenced finance-modelling depth; AI-governance domain doesn't pull on his crypto/community spikes.
apply_verdict: Apply — top priority for the warm-path alone. Craft a tight 300-word pitch leading with first-employee/seed-to-Series-A operator credibility, fundraising-support and strategy-doc track record, and AI-native (Claude Code) building. Send to rajen@governr.ai.
availability: live (direct founder email apply; 48h response window)
estimation_method: Two corroborating STALENESS signals, no exact date. (1) LinkedIn original (job 4315378460) now 301-redirects to '?trk=expired_jd_redirect' => LinkedIn posting has EXPIRED. LinkedIn jobs auto-expire ~30 days after posting (or on close) => posted >~4-5wk ago at minimum, likely older. (2) ZipRecruiter ID for CoS (453289607) is ~47 MILLION lower than governr's newer 'Agentic Developer' role (500524251) => CoS was posted SUBSTANTIALLY earlier in the same company's hiring sequence. (3) governr.ai site content indexed ~2026-03-13 (Google). Triangulation: expired LinkedIn + much-older zip ID + March site indexing => posting is old, estimate 8-12wk. SEPARATELY availability is likely still-open: ZipRecruiter + StudySmarter mirrors still resolve and apply is direct-to-founder email (rajen@governr.ai, 48h response) — an evergreen email channel that outlives the dated listing.
recency_note: STALE POSTING (med confidence): LinkedIn expired (expired_jd_redirect) + ZipRecruiter ID ~47M below governr's newer Agentic Developer role both indicate the CoS was posted well before the newer role; site content indexed ~Mar 2026. Estimate 8-12wk -> G_recency=18. IMPORTANT: stale LISTING != closed SEAT — direct founder-email apply (rajen@governr.ai) is evergreen and the warm-path (E=95) remains the strongest lever; verify the seat is still open in the 300-word email itself. Callback_v2 58 -> 51 (uses A_requirements_fit=50, E_warm_path=95 keys).
momentum_signals: VERY EARLY / THIN PUBLIC FOOTPRINT: governr (founder Rajen Madan) is a pre-seed/seed AI-governance & risk startup. NO funding round announcement found in public news/Crunchbase as of Jun 2026 - either unannounced or pre-institutional. Low data => momentum_confidence low.

RECENT PRODUCT MOTION (positive): soft-launched a scoped product 'AI Atlas' for mid-tier regulated firms (maps AI estate in ~2hr, live proof of control). Indicates a live, shipping company rather than dormant - but no dated press release found.

MARKET TAILWIND (positive, indirect): EU AI Act high-risk enforcement deadline Aug 2026 is driving procurement-led demand for AI-governance tooling - favourable timing for governr's category (newmarketpitch, dataversity).

HIRING SEQUENCE: governr has posted multiple roles (CoS + newer 'Agentic Developer', ZipRecruiter IDs ~47M apart) => company IS adding seats over time = alive and growing headcount, though slowly/early.
NO RED FLAGS but NO strong active-hiring signal: no founder 'we're scaling' post dated, no LinkedIn cadence/headcount data captured, no layoffs/pivot. Neutral by default; thin evidence => H=48.
CANDIDATE TREATMENT (positive process signal): founder promises 48h response to direct rajen@governr.ai applications - a responsive, non-ghosting signal even if overall momentum data is thin.
operator_fit_note: YES - founder-level generalist ownership (financial model + investor pitch + workflow design + strategy doc in one day) at an early AI co with direct-founder apply; maps strongly to Ryan's FA/fundraising-ops/ambiguity-to-structure spikes. Standalone financial-modelling depth is the one stretch.
momentum_note: Neutral / low-confidence. governr is very early with a thin public footprint - no announced raise, but a shipping product (AI Atlas), a favourable EU AI Act tailwind, an expanding role count, and a responsive direct-founder process. No red flags. H=48 (thin data band). callback_v2 51 -> callback_v3 51 (unchanged; neutral momentum neither helps nor hurts).

signals: PRIMARY LISTING EXPIRED (defining negative): the original LinkedIn CoS posting (job 4315378460) 301-redirects to '?trk=expired_jd_redirect' (re-confirmed live 2026-06-11) — LinkedIn has formally EXPIRED this posting. ZipRecruiter mirror (453289607) now returns HTTP 403, and the StudySmarter mirror 404s. No live, dated, refreshable listing remains on any board.

NO OWN-SITE CAREERS PAGE: governr.ai/careers and /jobs both return HTTP 404, and the homepage (HTTP 200) contains ZERO careers/hiring/'we're hiring'/CoS links or ATS embed. The company has no active hiring surface of its own for this seat.

NO FRESH FOUNDER/SOCIAL HIRING SIGNAL: WebSearch (founder Rajen Madan) surfaced no dated 'we're hiring a Chief of Staff' post; no recent funding announcement (pre-seed/seed, unannounced) to trigger a hiring push. Last datable footprint is ~March 2026 site indexing.

ONLY EVERGREEN CHANNEL REMAINS: the warm path is a direct 300-word email to rajen@governr.ai (48h response promise) — this is evergreen and outlives the dead listing, so the SEAT may still be fillable, but there is NO current evidence it is being actively recruited. Treat as DORMANT/quiet: apply via email to test live status, do not assume an active search.

MILD OFFSET: governr has posted multiple roles over time (CoS + newer 'Agentic Developer', ZipRec IDs ~47M apart) so the company is alive and adding seats — but the CoS req specifically is the OLDER, now-expired one. Net active-THIS-seat signal is weak.
